mission.title: Install and Configure KubeEdge on Kubernetes
mission.description: KubeEdge is a Kubernetes Native Edge Computing Framework that extends container orchestration and device management capabilities to edge hosts. By installing KubeEdge, you can manage edge applications and devices seamlessly using Kubernetes APIs, enhancing data processing and privacy at the edge.

steps.title: Install KubeEdge CloudCore via Helm
steps.description:
Add the official KubeEdge Helm repository and install CloudCore on the cloud-side Kubernetes cluster:

```bash
helm repo add kubeedge https://kubeedge.io/charts
helm repo update
```

Install CloudCore in the kubeedge namespace. Replace the advertise address with your cloud node's externally reachable IP:

```bash
helm install cloudcore kubeedge/cloudcore \
  --namespace kubeedge \
  --create-namespace \
  --set cloudCore.modules.cloudHub.advertiseAddress[0]="YOUR-CLOUD-NODE-IP"
```

**Alternative: Install using keadm CLI**

Download keadm and initialize the cloud side:

```bash
keadm init --advertise-address="YOUR-CLOUD-NODE-IP" --kubeedge-version=v1.21.0
```

steps.title: Verify the CloudCore installation
steps.description:
Check that CloudCore pods are running:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeedge
```

You should see the cloudcore pod in Running state.

steps.title: Get the join token
steps.description:
Retrieve the token needed for edge nodes to join the cluster:

```bash
keadm gettoken
```

Save this token for the next step.

steps.title: Join edge nodes
steps.description:
On each edge node, run keadm join with the token from the cloud side. Cloud and edge versions must match:

```bash
keadm join --cloudcore-ipport="YOUR-CLOUD-NODE-IP:10000" \
  --token=<TOKEN> \
  --kubeedge-version=v1.21.0
```

steps.title: Verify edge nodes
steps.description:
Check that the edge nodes have joined the cluster:

```bash
kubectl get nodes
```

Edge nodes will appear with the role 'edge'.

uninstall.title: Remove edge nodes
uninstall.description:
On each edge node, reset the edge side:

```bash
keadm reset
```

uninstall.title: Remove CloudCore via Helm
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the CloudCore release:

```bash
helm uninstall cloudcore --namespace kubeedge
```

Or if installed via keadm:

```bash
keadm reset --kube-config=/root/.kube/config
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove KubeEdge CRDs and the namespace:

```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep kubeedge.io | awk '{print $1}')
kubectl delete namespace kubeedge
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade CloudCore
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repo and upgrade CloudCore:

```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade cloudcore kubeedge/cloudcore --namespace kubeedge
```

Note: Cloud and edge versions must match. Upgrade edge nodes after the cloud side.

upgrade.title: Upgrade edge nodes
upgrade.description:
On each edge node, upgrade the edge side to match the cloud version:

```bash
keadm upgrade edge --kubeedge-version=<NEW_VERSION>
```

troubleshooting.title: CloudCore pod not starting
troubleshooting.description:
Check the CloudCore pod logs for errors:

```bash
kubectl logs -l app=cloudcore -n kubeedge
kubectl describe pod -l app=cloudcore -n kubeedge
```

troubleshooting.title: Edge node cannot join
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure the advertise address is reachable from the edge node, and ports 10000-10004 are open. Verify the token is correct:

```bash
keadm gettoken
```

troubleshooting.title: Version mismatch
troubleshooting.description:
Cloud and edge versions must match. Check versions on both sides:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeedge -o yaml | grep image:
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KubeEdge will have CloudCore running in the kubeedge namespace on the cloud side, and EdgeCore running on each edge node. Edge nodes will appear in kubectl get nodes with the 'edge' role.

prerequisites.description: Requires a Kubernetes cluster on the cloud side, Helm and kubectl installed. The keadm CLI is needed for joining edge nodes. Edge nodes require root access.
